behavior_summary: AMOSTAT::look handles the game's 'U8P_AMOSTAT::look' (inspect) interaction for the Amoras statue/object.

When a player (pid) looks at the object (Event 0), the script reads the global flag ancientOnesMet and chooses between two short item bark strings: if ancientOnesMet == 0 it requests Item::bark to display "Amoras", otherwise it requests Item::bark to display "female statue ".
The script does not modify any global flags; it calls Item::bark, captures its return, performs the implied/pop flow (engine dialog/flow handling), then suspends/returns.
notable_control_flow: Entry on Event 0 (U8P_AMOSTAT::look) -> read global flag ancientOnesMet -> conditional branch selecting one of two static strings -> call Item::bark(pid, string) on the item instance -> capture return value (retval) and drive implies/pop sequence -> suspend/return.
No global writes or state changes performed by this script; it is purely descriptive/response logic for inspection.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the string "female statue " intentional for downstream concatenation/formatting, or an artifact of extraction? (confidence: medium)
Are there additional events or states where AMOSTAT has different behavior (other events than Event 0) not present in this disassembly? (confidence: low)
What higher-level quest or dialog logic sets ancientOnesMet and how that affects other objects besides AMOSTAT is outside the scope of this script. (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_AMOSTAT::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examination (U8P_AMOSTAT::look) description for the Amoras statue/item, selecting one of two brief bark messages depending on whether the Ancient Ones have been met.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_AMOSTAT::look) — triggered when a player/actor looks at or inspects the object.
Invocation with parameters: pid (viewer/process id) and item pointer/this (object instance at BP+06h).

function_map.name: U8P_AMOSTAT::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_AMOSTAT::look' (examine) event for the Amoras statue; choose description based on ancientOnesMet and call Item::bark.

external_calls.purpose: Request the item to display a short description/message to the specified player/process (pid). Called with pid and a pointer to the chosen static string.

reads.name: ancientOnesMet
reads.likely_effect: Guards which bark string to display: 0 -> "Amoras", otherwise -> "female statue ". Does not get modified by this script.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: AMOSTAT::look handles the game's 'U8P_AMOSTAT::look' (inspect) interaction for the Amoras statue/object.
